title: Breakwave Tanker Report 1.27.2026
summary:
Main
in Weekly Shipbuilding Reports 27/01/2026
VLCC Rates Soften but Remain at Historically High Levels - The dirty tanker market continues to trade at substantially higher year-over-year
Source: Breakwave Advisors

Linked asset: 2026-01-27_BreakwaveTankersJanuary272026Report.pdf
Source asset: reports/hellenic/shipbuilding/pdfs/2026-01-27_BreakwaveTankersJanuary272026Report.pdf

[Page 1]
Tanker Shipping
Bi-Weekly Industry Report
January 27, 2026
• VLCC Rates Soften but Remain at Historically High Levels - The dirty tanker
Short-term Indicators
market continues to trade at substantially higher year-over-year levels, with the
Momentum: Positive
Baltic Dirty Tanker Index currently around 90% above the same period last year. This underscores that, in aggregate, freight rates across dirty tanker routes remain Sentiment: Positive
well above historical norms, despite recent short-term adjustments in spot markets. Fundamentals: Positive
Toward the end of January, market indicators point to softer momentum in VLCC
spot rates, reflected in negative week-over-week movements on key benchmark
routes.
